heroCases.title: Entrapment - Riverside Medical Centre Elevator 3
heroCases.description: Patient transport staff member trapped in Elevator 3 between floors 2 and 3. Bed elevator essential for patient movement. Hospital security on scene. Staff member is calm but concerned about patients waiting for transport.

timeline.subject: Initial entrapment report
timeline.notes: David Chen (Facilities Manager) reported staff member trapped in Elevator 3. Hospital security on scene. Staff member responsive. This is a bed elevator - patient transport affected.

timeline.subject: Technician Dispatch - EMERGENCY
timeline.notes: Healthcare SLA - 1 hour response. Contacted Manchester dispatch. Ian Williams (Tech ID: OT-UK-2847) is 8 minutes away at nearby clinic. Rerouting immediately.

timeline.subject: Technician ETA - Elevator 3 Entrapment - PRIORITY RESPONSE
timeline.body: Mr. Chen, Technician Ian Williams is en route. ETA 8 minutes. Please ensure security maintains intercom contact with the trapped person. We'll call back in 5 minutes with update. Given this is a bed elevator, we're treating this as highest priority.

timeline.subject: 5-minute update call
timeline.notes: Called David to confirm tech is 3 min out. Staff member calm. Hospital has rerouted patient transport to Elevator 4 temporarily.

heroCases.demoNotes: PRIMARY HERO CASE - Use this for incoming call simulation. Shows healthcare priority handling, screen pop with critical account context, and emergency dispatch. Great for demonstrating SLA urgency.

heroCases.title: Recurring Door Issue - Canary Wharf Tower A Elevator 12
heroCases.description: Third report this month of intermittent door closing issues on SkyRise elevator #12. Door reverses multiple times before closing, causing significant delays during morning rush. Tenants in the tower complaining loudly.

timeline.subject: RE: Elevator 12 Door Issue - 3rd occurrence this month
timeline.body: Team, this is now the third time this month we've had door issues on E12. Major tenant JPMorgan on floors 35-40 has complained formally. We need a permanent fix, not just adjustments. Please escalate.

timeline.subject: Case Review - Historical Analysis
timeline.notes: Pulled maintenance history. Two prior tickets (CAS-00798, CAS-00815) for same unit. Both times tech adjusted door operator parameters. Recurring issue suggests possible door edge sensor degradation or track alignment problem.

timeline.subject: RE: Elevator 12 Door Issue - Escalation & Comprehensive Inspection
timeline.body: Sophie, I've escalated this to our Field Supervisor. We're scheduling a comprehensive door system inspection including sensor replacement and track realignment. Proposed date: March 17th during low-traffic hours (6-8 AM). Given the tenant concerns, I've also flagged this for senior management review.

timeline.subject: Scheduling confirmation and scope expansion
timeline.notes: Sophie confirmed March 17th works. Requested we also check units #10 and #11 in same bank while onsite. Added to work order scope. She mentioned JPMorgan lease renewal coming up - this is sensitive.

knowledgeArticles.title: Door Edge Sensor Replacement - Step by Step

heroCases.demoNotes: RECURRING ISSUE CASE - Shows historical case linking, pattern recognition, proactive escalation, and scheduled maintenance coordination. Good for demonstrating agent research capabilities and Similar Cases in Copilot.

heroCases.title: Modernization Inquiry - The Shard Freight Elevators
heroCases.description: Customer interested in modernization options for their freight elevators. Looking to improve energy efficiency and add IoT monitoring capabilities. Requesting sales consultation.

timeline.subject: New case from customer portal
timeline.notes: Customer submitted inquiry via self-service portal. Attached PDF with their modernization requirements document.

timeline.subject: Initial Review
timeline.notes: Reviewed requirements doc. Customer wants: 1) Regenerative drives for energy savings, 2) Otis ONE IoT monitoring, 3) Updated cab interiors. 3 freight units at approximately £150K each = £450K project opportunity. The Shard is a flagship account.

heroCases.demoNotes: SALES HANDOFF CASE - Shows service-to-sales flow, attachment handling, opportunity creation, and consultation scheduling. Good for demonstrating cross-department collaboration and case routing.

heroCases.title: Billing Dispute - Marriott Hotel Extra Charges
heroCases.description: Customer disputing charges on recent invoice. Claims they were billed for repairs that should be covered under their service contract. Contract renewal coming up in 60 days - risk of non-renewal if not handled properly.

timeline.subject: Billing dispute - Invoice #INV-2026-03-4521
timeline.notes: Sarah Jenkins called regarding £1,800 charge for door operator motor replacement on Lift 2. Claims this should be covered under Full Maintenance contract. Reviewed contract - she's correct, door operators ARE covered. This appears to be a billing error. Sarah mentioned contract renewal coming up and is considering competitors.

timeline.subject: Investigation - Billing Review
timeline.notes: Contacted billing department. Invoice was generated from WO-2026-03982 which was incorrectly coded as Time & Materials instead of Contract work. Submitted correction request CR-78451.

timeline.subject: RE: Invoice Dispute - Credit Pending
timeline.body: Ms. Jenkins, Thank you for bringing this to our attention. You are correct that door operator repairs are covered under your Full Maintenance contract. We've identified a coding error and submitted a credit request for the full £1,800. You should see the credit on your next statement. I've also looped in your Account Manager, David Kim, who would like to schedule a contract review call before your May renewal.

timeline.subject: Escalation - Account Manager Engaged
timeline.notes: Account Manager David Kim reached out. Scheduling lunch meeting with Sarah to discuss renewal and offer service upgrade as goodwill gesture for the billing error.

heroCases.demoNotes: ESCALATED BILLING CASE - Shows financial dispute handling, contract verification, credit processing, and account manager coordination. Good for demonstrating escalation workflows and customer retention scenarios.

heroCases.title: Preventive Maintenance Callback - Westfield London
heroCases.description: Tenant coordinator following up on scheduled maintenance for escalators. Wants to confirm scope of work and coordinate building access for technician team ahead of busy weekend shopping period.

transcript.message: Hi, this is James from Westfield London. We have escalator PM scheduled for Wednesday and I need to confirm the details before the weekend rush.
Hi James! Happy to help. Let me pull up your scheduled maintenance. One moment please.
I see you have preventive maintenance scheduled for Wednesday March 19th. The work will cover all 24 escalators in the main shopping areas. We'll have a team of 4 technicians on site from 6 AM.
Perfect. Will any escalators be offline during shopping hours?

We'll complete most inspections before the centre opens at 10 AM. For any that need extended attention, we'll work on them in pairs so there's always an adjacent escalator running. Estimated completion by 2 PM.
Excellent. Can you send me the scope of work and lead technician details for our security team?
Absolutely! I'll email you the PM checklist, technician roster, and their ID photos for security clearance right now. Is james.morrison@westfield.com still the best email?
Yes that's correct. Also please copy our security desk at security@westfield.com
Done! Email sent to both addresses. Anything else you need before Wednesday?
That's everything. Thanks for the quick help!
You're welcome! Have a great rest of your day, and we'll see you Wednesday.

timeline.subject: Westfield London - Escalator PM Scope & Technician Details - March 19
timeline.body:
James,

As discussed, attached is the PM scope of work for your 24 NCE escalators.

Scheduled: Wednesday, March 19, 2026 @ 6:00 AM
Estimated Completion: 2:00 PM
Lead Technician: Rachel Green
Team Size: 4 technicians

Attached:
- PM Checklist
- Technician roster with ID photos
- Building access requirements

Please ensure loading dock access is available for our service vehicle from 5:30 AM.

Best regards

heroCases.demoNotes: CHAT CHANNEL CASE - Shows chat transcript preservation, omnichannel experience, and proactive customer coordination. Good for demonstrating chat handling, email follow-up workflows, and multi-contact communication.
